title: Can new pills end sleeping sickness?
description: European drug regulators on Friday endorsed a new and simpler treatment for sleeping sickness, in what could be a giant boost to efforts to eliminate the disease. A European Medicines Agency committee gave its nod to acoziborole, developed by Sanofi and DNDi. Speaking to France 24, Dr Luis Pizarro, executive director of DNDi, said the new pill could revolutionise treatment of the disease, which has killed hundreds of thousands across Africa.

Israeli activities last week were relatively more intense than in preceding weeks and included attacks on Hezbollah assets deep inside Lebanon, some of which the group reportedly intended to use against Israel in the event of a US conflict with Iran.

title: Apache Helicopters Get New “Drone-Killer” 30mm Proximity-Fused Ammunition
description: Live-fire trials of the AH-64 with XM1225 APEX ammunition come as both U.S. and Russian attack helicopters experiment with proximity-fused cannon rounds to defeat small UAVs. The U.S. Army conducted the first successful live fire test of the 30113 mm XM1225 Aviation Proximity Explosive (APEX) ammunition, employed by an AH-64 Apache attack helicopter in an []

title: Ukrainian Air Force F-16 Captured Shooting Down Russian Target with APKWS II
description: Following the first sighting of the new capability in December 2025, a Ukrainian F-16 was captured using APKWS II laser-guided rockets to engage a drone. A Ukrainian Air Force F-16 was captured for the first time while using its Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System (APKWS) II guided rockets against a Russian unmanned system, believed to []

title: British chefs in Paris shake up old food stereotypes
description: From sausage rolls and Scotch eggs to daring, ingredient-led tasting menus, a new generation of British chefs is turning heads and changing minds in Paris. Long mocked for beans on toast and beige plates, UK cuisine is getting a stylish reboot in the French capital, where creativity, French technique and bold reinvention are helping British food shed its clichés and win over one of the world’s toughest culinary crowds.

title: Signs of 'gradual progress' in Iran-US talks
description: Iran said Friday that in order to reach a deal, the United States will have to drop its "excessive demands", tempering the optimism expressed after talks seen as a last-ditch bid to avert war. Nevertheless there are signs of "gradual progress" being made towards a deal on Iran's nuclear enrichment programme, says France 24's Philip Turle.

title: 'Clashes still continuing' on parts of Pakistan-Afghanistan border
description: The situation remains tense and clashes are continuing along some parts of the Pakistan-Afghanistan border, reports France 24's correspondent Shahzaib Wahlah, after Afghanistan launched a cross-border retaliatory attack on Pakistan overnight that saw Islamabad hit back with airstrikes on Kabul.

title: Sweden says jammed suspected Russian drone near French warship
description: Sweden says its armed forces spotted and jammed a drone near the French aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle during a stopover in Malmo on Wednesday, with the country’s defence minister saying the drone was “probably” of Russian origin.

title: Did Epstein help Israel push for a security deal with Ivory Coast?
description: Latest Epstein files released in January reveal the extent of his relations with some prominent African leaders.

title: South Sudan at risk of ‘return to full-scale war’, UN warns
description: UN report warns of escalating atrocity risks amid renewed fighting and widespread impunity over abuses.

title: Drone jammed near French aircraft carrier was probably Russian, says Sweden
description: Sweden's defence minister says there is likely a "strong link" between the drone and a Russian naval vessel in the area.

title: At least 55 Ghanaians killed in Russia-Ukraine war, minister says
description: This is the highest number of confirmed casualties from a single African country in the Ukraine-Russia war.

title: What we know after latest escalation in Pakistan-Afghanistan tensions
description: Bombings by Pakistan are the most significant development in tensions between the two neighbours - though the physical impact remains unclear.

title: Why Are Pakistan and Afghanistan Fighting?
description: The renewed violence between the neighboring countries stems from Pakistan’s accusations that Afghanistan’s Taliban government has harbored a militant group.

title: With a New Nigeria Refinery, Africa’s Richest Man Wants ‘to Rescue the Country’
description: Aliko Dangote, Africa’s richest man, has an ambitious vision for the continent’s most populous nation, but hurdles stand in his way.

title: Pakistan Strikes Afghanistan in ‘Open War’ Against Taliban Regime
description: The airstrikes came hours after Afghan troops had attacked Pakistani border positions and follow months of worsening relations between the neighboring countries.

title: A Trump Call Ignited Saudi-U.A.E. Feud
description: A request made to President Trump about the war in Sudan is at the heart of a diplomatic dispute between Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.
